name: Bahr ibn Kab
title: Looter of the Imam's Clothes
shortDescription: One of the vilest individuals in Umar Sa'd's army who, after Imam Hussain's (AS) martyrdom, looted his trousers, leaving his body exposed.
fullStory:
Bahr ibn Ka'b (or Abjar ibn Ka'b) was one of the most despicable and shameless soldiers in the Kufan army, whose eyes were blinded by worldly greed.

Before going to the battlefield, to prevent his clothes from being looted and his body left naked after martyrdom, Imam Hussain (AS) asked for an old garment (or torn trousers) and wore it beneath his main clothes.

However, after the Imam's martyrdom, at the height of the enemy's cruelty, various people looted the Imam's clothes, armor, and weapons. Amidst this, Bahr ibn Ka'b advanced with utmost shamelessness and cruelty, stripping even that old garment and trousers from the blood-drenched body of the Imam.

Due to this great sin and shameless disrespect to God's vicegerent, he suffered a severe curse and torment in this very world. Historical accounts state that Bahr ibn Ka'b's hands would become as dry as wood in winter, and in summer they would become so moist that blood and pus dripped from them.
quoteArabic: وَسَلَبَ سَراوِيلَهُ بَحْرُ بْنُ كَعْبٍ، فَتَرَكَهُ مُجَرَّداً...
quote: (Maqtal narrative of the Imam's peak oppression:) And his trousers were looted by Bahr ibn Ka'b, leaving him bare...
